concerns.text: The plan identifies a 'Fatal Flaw' where the verified ROI datasets (Koch test/classification) lack 'belief ratings' and 'CRT scores', and proposes generating these as 'synthetic variables' to run the pipeline. This renders the analysis incapable of answering the research question (impact of visual attention on susceptibility). A regression model with synthetic outcomes and moderators tests only the code's ability to run, not the psychological hypothesis. The plan must either identify a real dataset containing all three modalities (eye-tracking, belief ratings, CRT) or explicitly reframe the research question to match the available data (e.g., correlation between fixation and task accuracy if available), rather than simulating the core variables.

The 'NRC Emotion Lexicon' dataset cited (chuuhtetnaing/myanmar-nrc-format-dataset) appears to be specific to the Myanmar language. The spec and plan imply a general English headline analysis (referencing VADER and standard NRC). If the headline corpus is English, this dataset is inappropriate. If the corpus is Myanmar, the plan must explicitly state this and ensure the 'Koch' eye-tracking datasets also contain Myanmar stimuli to maintain modality alignment.
